query: Sundar Pichai contempt of court Mumbai YouTube video details charges 2026

mako.raw:
{"query":"Sundar Pichai contempt of court Mumbai YouTube video details charges 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1353,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.livemint.com/news/india/google-ceo-sundar-pichai-served-contempt-notice-from-mumbai-court-over-defamatory-youtube-video-11733128846388.html","title":"Google CEO Sundar Pichai served contempt notice by Mumbai Court over defamatory YouTube video | Today News","content":"Google's CEO Sundar Pichai served a contempt notice as subsidiary YouTube fails to comply with the removal of a video, reported multiple media outlets.
The notice issued on November 21 by the Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate’s Court in Ballard Pier said the entertainment platform failed to comply with a March 2023 order to remove a defamatory video targeting the Dhyan Foundation and its founder, Yogi Ashwini, reported the news portal Business Today.
The Dhyan Foundation also filed a contempt plea on YouTube in October 2022.
They also alleged that the YouTube video “Pakhandi Baba ki Kartut” is accessible outside India despite the court's removal order."},{"url":"https://m.economictimes.com/news/india/mumbai-court-issues-contempt-notice-to-sundar-pichai/articleshow/115816014.cms","title":"Mumbai court issues contempt notice to Sundar Pichai - The Economic Times","content":"An Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate's Court in Ballard Pier on November 21 asked Pichai why contempt proceedings should not be initiated against him for YouTube’s failure to remove a defamatory video titled “Pakhandi Baba ki Kartut” from its platform, as directed in March last year.
The contempt case has been posted for further hearing on January 3.
Dhyan Foundation, which claims to be dedicated to animal welfare, had filed a contempt petition claiming that the court’s March 31 takedown order had not been complied by the platform, thus contempt should be initiated against the CEO for non-compliance with the order.
However, the video is still visible outside India on YouTube and everyone can watch it, it said, adding that Google “intentionally and deliberately” did not remove the video which contained false and malicious allegations tarnishing its reputation.
The platform stated that such grievances should be addressed in civil courts, not criminal courts."},{"url":"https://www.business-standard.com/india-news/mumbai-court-slaps-contempt-notice-on-google-ceo-sundar-pichai-here-s-why-124120200533_1.html","title":"Mumbai court slaps contempt notice on Google CEO Sundar Pichai; here's why | India News - Business Standard","content":"A Mumbai court today issued a contempt notice against Google CEO Sundar Pichai.
Dhyan Foundation alleges the video contains defamatory and obscene material that has harmed the organisation’s reputation and that of Yogi Ashwini.
In response, YouTube has invoked its immunity under the Information Technology (IT) Act, stating that defamation does not fall within the categories of content that can be blocked under Section 69-A of the Act."},{"url":"https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/mumbai-court-issues-contempt-notice-to-google-ceo-sundar-pichai-over-pakhandi-baba-ki-kartut-video/articleshow/115847182.cms","title":"YouTube removes 'Pakhandi Baba Ki Kartut' video in India following court order, contempt case pending - The Times of India","content":"The order, issued on November 21, 2023, stems from YouTube’s non-removal of a defamatory video titled "Pakhandi Baba ki Kartut" which targets the Dhyan Foundation and its founder, Yogi Ashwini.
Despite an earlier directive in March 2022 to remove the video, it remains accessible outside India, prompting legal action against Google for non-compliance.
This move highlights the court's dissatisfaction with YouTube's lack of adherence to the order.While the contempt plea was filed in October last year, notice was issued last week.
This time over a complaint filed by gaming company WinZO.
This follows previous penalties imposed by the Competition Commission of India (CCI) in 2022 for abusing its dominant position in the Android mobile ecosystem and Play Store."}]}
